response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack software engineer with a strong background in web, mobile, and embedded systems development, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, deep learning workload analysis, and GPU/AI accelerator architecture — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest relevant work is low-latency application development at Sociomantic using async I/O in DLang, but this is application-layer performance, not hardware or silicon-level optimization. His AI exposure is limited to MCP agent tooling at the application layer, not DNN training/inference optimization. The educational background (BS in CS, BS in Fine Arts) does not meet the preferred academic profile for a principal-level hardware architecture role.
strengths: Long tenure (30+ years) in software engineering demonstrates deep technical experience and adaptability across domains
Low-latency systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, high-throughput microservices) shows some performance-oriented engineering instincts
Contributions to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling indicate awareness of modern AI ecosystem and tooling patterns
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or any hardware-level performance work — the core requirement of this role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or GPU architecture workload analysis
Role requires principal/senior IC hardware expertise; candidate's entire career is in web/full-stack software engineering with no hardware-software co-design work

key_factors.factor: No deep learning or GPU architecture background
